## Slides: From biological molecule to sequencing data

Any step can add bias or discard information.

```text
Biological sample
  ↓
Extracted DNA/RNA molecules
  ↓
Library with adapters/barcodes
  ↓
Raw instrument measurements
  ↓
Basecalled reads
  ↓
Read alignments or assemblies
  ↓
Variants, annotations, and graphs
  ↓
Biological interpretation
```

::: {.notes}
Use this chain throughout the lecture. Ask participants where molecule length, PCR bias, basecalling, mapping bias, and graph construction enter.
:::

## Slides: A sequence read is an inference

- **Molecule:** physical DNA or RNA entering preparation
- **Library:** prepared, adapted, and possibly amplified molecules
- **Read:** inferred sequence emitted by an instrument and basecaller
- **Coverage:** how much evidence supports a base, molecule, haplotype, k-mer, or graph path
- **Metadata:** context required to interpret the observation


## Slides: Quality scores

$$Q = -10\log_{10}(P_{error})$$

| Score | Approximate error probability |
|---:|---:|
| Q20 | 1 in 100 |
| Q30 | 1 in 1,000 |
| Q40 | 1 in 10,000 |

Accuracy alone does not determine biological usefulness.


## Slides: The exact meaning of coverage depends on what is being counted

| Coverage type | What is counted |
|---|---|
| Read depth | Reads overlapping a base or region |
| Physical coverage | Original molecules spanning a region |
| Haplotype coverage | Evidence assigned to each chromosome copy |
| K-mer coverage | Occurrences of a sequence word |
| Graph coverage | Evidence supporting nodes, edges, or paths |

> “30x” average read depth does not mean every object is observed 30 times.

## Slides: Read depth varies along a sequence

```text
Reference:  A C G T A C G T A C G T
Read 1:     A C G T A C
Read 2:         G T A C G T
Read 3:             A C G T A C

Depth:      1 1 2 2 3 3 2 2 1 1 0 0
```
Average depth summarizes the dataset; local depth is what supports a particular base or region.


## Slides: Expected average read depth at sequencing

For a genome of length `G`, sequenced with `N` reads of average length `L`

```text
expected average read depth = (N x L) / G
```
Example:  `1,000,000 reads x 150 bp / 5,000,000 bp = 30x` average depth. 

>> 30x average depth does not guarantee 30 reads at every base! 
*Repeats, GC bias, mapping ambiguity, and random sampling create uneven depth* 

## Slides: Physical coverage

Counts how many original DNA molecules or library fragments span a position or region

```text
Five overlapping fragments all span the same region, giving a physical coverage of 5×

[F1 --->]-------------------------[<--- R1]
[F2 --->]----------------------------[<--- R2]
   [F3 --->]-------------------------[<--- R3]
      [F4 --->]----------------------[<--- R4]
         [F5 --->]---------------------[<--- R5]
|<-------------- 500 bp region ------------------>|
```

## Slides:  Haplotype coverage (i) 

Evidence assigned to each chromosome copy or haplotype

```text
Reference position:              *              Ten reads overlap this
                                 |              heterozygous position.
Haplotype 1 reads:  -----A-------|   6 reads    Six carry A, four carry G,
                    -----A-------|              total read depth is 10x.
                    -----A-------|              
                    -----A-------|              
                    -----A-------|              
                    -----A-------|             When alleles are phased,    
                                               haplotype depths are 6x and 4x.
Haplotype 2 reads:  -----G-------|   4 reads
                    -----G-------|
                    -----G-------|
                    -----G-------|

Total depth:                       10x
Haplotype 1 depth:                  6x
Haplotype 2 depth:                  4x
```

## Slides:  Haplotype coverage (ii)

Haplotype coverage matters for phasing and diploid assembly

```text
Haplotype 1:  A -------- C -------- G
Haplotype 2:  G -------- T -------- A

Long read:    A -------- C -------- G   -> supports haplotype 1
```


## Slides:  Graph path coverage

How much read, assembly, or haplotype evidence supports a node, edge, or complete route through a genome graph

```text
                     /-- B --\
graph path:    A ---<         >--- D
                     \-- C --/

Reads supporting A-B-D: 18
Reads supporting A-C-D: 12
```
- **Node coverage:** reads or bases aligned to a node.
- **Edge coverage:** reads or paths supporting a connection.
- **Path coverage:** reads, assemblies, or haplotypes supporting a route.
